questions.text: What is the closed-form matrix solution for Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) regression, assuming the feature matrix X has full column rank?
options: w = (X^T * X + lambda * I)^-1 * X^T * y
questions.explanation: Setting the gradient of the sum of squared residuals to zero yields the normal equations X^T * X * w = X^T * y. Inverting X^T * X yields the OLS weight vector.

questions.text: Maximizing the posterior probability (MAP) of a linear regression model under the assumption of independent, zero-mean Laplace priors on the weights is equivalent to which regularization scheme?
questions.explanation: A Laplace distribution prior contains an absolute value term |w_j| in its exponent. Taking the negative logarithm converts this multiplication into the sum of absolute values, which corresponds exactly to the L1 norm penalty used in LASSO regression.
